frame.mexico_role: Mexico <HS cells are composition weights — not pooled with H-1B / college+ fiscal paths
frame.dimensionality: education_bucket × state × descendants; H-1B / STEM as separate bucket from <HS Mexico surge cells
frame.labor_fiscal_bridge: High-skill immigration GE gains (Ottaviano-Peri, Bound-Khanna) do not translate 1:1 to NAS NPV rows — innovation spillovers, occupational mobility, and distributional splits omitted
frame.tension: Aggregate native wage +1.8% (O-P) coexists with CS wage −2.6–5.1% (H-1B) and U-shaped occupational downgrade on arrival (Chiswick DP452)

extraction_notes.bound_w23153: GE model 1994–2001 IT boom; H-1B cap binds; innovation spillover parameter β calibrated ≈0.23.
extraction_notes.ottaviano_w12497: 1990–2004 immigration; σ (native-immigrant substitutability) ≈0.13–0.21; revises Borjas partial-equilibrium losses.
extraction_notes.iza_dp452: Longitudinal Survey of Immigrants to Australia; ANU3 occupational prestige; U-shaped assimilation by visa class.

parameter_claims.population: US computer scientists, counterfactual vs 1994 H-1B stock
parameter_claims.notes: λ (labor demand elasticity) sensitivity; native CS harmed, economy-wide native utility still up.
Partial-equilibrium crowd-out; mitigated by occupational switching in GE extension.

parameter_claims.population: US IT goods output / consumer prices
parameter_claims.notes: Consumer benefit channel absent from fiscal NPV scalars; complements Peri task-specialization frame.
Without innovation spillovers (β=0) gain only 0.02–0.03%. CS stayers −5.13% utility; non-college +0.44%.

parameter_claims.population: US vs foreign-born within education-experience cells, 1960–2004
parameter_claims.notes: Imperfect substitutes because of occupational choice — directly supports separate education_bucket / occupation cells vs perfect-substitute NAS grouping.

parameter_claims.population: US-born workers overall, long run GE
parameter_claims.notes: Short run +0.7%; <HS native −1.1% long run / −2.2% short vs Borjas −4.2% / −8% partial methods.
Comparable to congruence between native HS dropouts and HS graduates (0.68) — immigrants not direct substitutes within education cell.
Last job pre-migration 67.96 → first AU job 55.78 → wave 3 49.82 (professionals); total sample 47.90 → 39.41 → 35.74. Validates arrival downgrade not captured in static NAS education bucket.

generators.name: H-1B bucket ≠ Mexico <HS NAS cell
generators.prompt: Split lifetime fiscal scenarios by admission channel: H-1B/STEM high-skill (Bound-Khanna) vs <HS unauthorized/surge (Hanson/CBO). Where does any memo pool them into one 'immigrant NPV'? Map H-1B to separate education_bucket with innovation/firm-profit offsets not in NAS Table 8-12.
generators.retrodiction: Would have blocked scalar Mexico verdict from absorbing H-1B federal-positive innovation path; CS wage −2.6–5.1% distributional fork explicit.
generators.negative_space: NAS education cells conflate arrival credential with visa/channel-specific fiscal and labor dynamics.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: College+ NPV applies to H-1B temporary workers
H-1B cap binding implies same fiscal sign as family-based <HS
Innovation spillovers appear in federal transfer ledger automatically

generators.name: Partial vs general equilibrium wage claims
generators.prompt: Tag each wage study as partial (same education cell competition) or GE (capital adjustment + cross-education complementarity). Ottaviano-Peri: σ≈0.13–0.21 → +1.8% avg native vs Borjas −3% partial. Bound-Khanna: CS harmed, non-college +0.44% utility. Which fiscal memos cite partial elasticities as lifetime verdict?
generators.retrodiction: Would have flagged Borjas-style −8% <HS partial estimates incompatible with O-P −2.2% short run when occupational differentiation allowed.
generators.negative_space: Lifetime fiscal synthesis cites wage losses without GE capital/complementarity or occupational reallocation.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Fixed capital short-run wage loss equals lifetime fiscal externality
Average native wage effect applies to each education_bucket row
generators.theory_tested: partial_wage_effect magnitude > GE wage effect for same education cell

generators.name: Occupational mobility vs education-at-arrival bucket
generators.prompt: Use Chiswick DP452 U-shaped ANU3 path: pre-migration prestige → arrival downgrade → partial recovery. Does NAS/education_bucket treat sheepskin at arrival as permanent skill proxy? Cross to Ottaviano-Peri occupational congruence 0.6–0.7. High-skilled visa (Business/Humanitarian) vs tied-migrant steepness.
generators.retrodiction: Would have warned that professionals arriving at 67.96 ANU3 score first job at 55.78 — lifetime NPV keyed on education alone misses assimilation dynamics and descendant occupational path.
generators.negative_space: Descendant band in scenario ledger has earnings projection but no occupational downgrade/recovery state.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Education at arrival equals lifetime productivity
Cross-sectional assimilation slope equals longitudinal U-shape
Australia LSI U-shape generalizes to US H-1B without visa-class mapping
generators.theory_tested: occupational_downgrade_depth inversely related to eventual fiscal contribution

generators.name: Innovation spillover vs substitute crowd-out (H-1B)
generators.prompt: Decompose Bound-Khanna counterfactual: β≈0.23 innovation spillover raises aggregate native utility to ~0.21% vs 0.02–0.03% without; IT profits +0.61–0.70%; CS stayers −5.13% utility. Which lifetime studies count patent/innovation fiscal externality? Separate firm-owner surplus from worker incidence.
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced that killing spillovers collapses H-1B welfare case — fiscal ledger needs explicit innovation row or H-1B bucket stays incomplete.
generators.negative_space: Federal positive NPV for college+ often assumes static payroll tax without IT sector profit/innovation feedback or CS distributional loss.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: H-1B innovators pay more in payroll than CS workers lose
Monopolistic competition profits are fiscally irrelevant
Outsourcing offset makes domestic CS crowd-out negligible

generators.name: State × education_bucket high-skill destination sorting
generators.prompt: Map H-1B/STEM concentration (CA/WA/NY/TX) vs <HS surge states. Ottaviano-Peri: 90% of natives have ≥HS and gain 0.7–3.4%. Bound-Khanna: non-college natives gain +0.44% utility from H-1B era GE. Build state×education_bucket×descendants cells — refuse national scalar.
generators.retrodiction: Would have prevented applying Mexico <HS state-local shock ledger to H-1B-heavy states where federal payroll and innovation channels dominate.
generators.negative_space: State fiscal capacity literature (BoC Presidio) rarely splits high-skill tech hubs from border <HS counties within same state.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: California H-1B fiscal sign equals Texas border county sign
Descendant education inherits parent bucket without mobility U-shape
College+ NAS NPV applies uniformly across states
generators.theory_tested: state_federal_proxy sign differs by education_bucket within same state

theories_tested.theory: H-1B / college+ bucket federal lifetime NPV should not inherit Mexico <HS scalar
theories_tested.prediction: npv_education_benchmarks row for HS/college+ differs in sign from <HS AND should carry channel tag H-1B vs family/surge
theories_tested.duckdb_test: SELECT study, acs_education_bucket, individual_npv_2012_usd, includes_descendants FROM npv_education_benchmarks WHERE acs_education_bucket IN ('<HS', 'HS / GED', 'BA+') ORDER BY acs_education_bucket, study
theories_tested.falsifier: Single pooled immigrant NPV scalar used in scenario_ledger without education_bucket dimension

theories_tested.theory: Imperfect native-immigrant substitutability (σ≈0.15) attenuates <HS wage loss used in fiscal arguments
theories_tested.prediction: GE-adjusted <HS native wage loss < partial-equilibrium Borjas-style loss magnitude
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Register O-P priors: compare npv_education_benchmarks <HS individual_npv against labor-market modules tagged partial_equilibrium; flag if memo cites >4% <HS wage loss without σ adjustment
theories_tested.falsifier: Warehouse wage shock tables apply perfect-substitute elasticity within education cells

theories_tested.theory: Occupational downgrade on arrival breaks education-at-arrival NAS cell for high-skill migrants
theories_tested.prediction: Professional immigrants face ≥10 ANU3 point drop at first destination job (Table 3: 67.96→55.78)
theories_tested.duckdb_test: JOIN origin_puma_household_context_2023 (education_bucket, origin_label) WITH scenario rows WHERE education_bucket IN ('BA+','Some college') AND arrival_occupation_premium_proxy IS NULL — count unmatched high-skill rows lacking mobility state
theories_tested.falsifier: All BA+ warehouse rows assume occupation equals education credential at t=0

theories_tested.theory: H-1B innovation spillover (β≈0.23) is load-bearing for positive native welfare in Bound-Khanna
theories_tested.prediction: Utility gain drops from ~0.21% to ~0.03% when spillover shut down — fiscal scenario must expose innovation sensitivity
theories_tested.duckdb_test: SELECT b.study, b.acs_education_bucket, b.individual_npv_2012_usd, b.adjustment FROM npv_education_benchmarks b WHERE b.acs_education_bucket NOT IN ('<HS') AND b.adjustment NOT LIKE '%innovation%' AND b.individual_npv_2012_usd > 0
theories_tested.falsifier: Positive college+ NPV robust in metadata without innovation/spillover adjustment tag
